app.title: Liqvia Treasury
app.description: 13-week cash forecast, liquidity intelligence, and executive CFO insights for SMEs.

feature.dashboard: See what's coming in, what's going out, and how much cash is safely yours to spend
feature.bankAccounts: Bank accounts with aggregate cash position & transactions
feature.planning: 13-week forecast, budget vs actual & AR/AP ledger
feature.aiCfo: AI CFO assistant grounded in your live financial data

landing.title: Cash Forecasting You Can Actually Trust
landing.titleSecondary: Know your cash position, runway and risks — every day.
landing.subtitle: Unlike AI tools that produce unexplained forecasts, Liqvia shows exactly where every forecast comes from.

hero.eyebrow: The Cash Operating System for SMEs
hero.supporting: Liqvia brings together your cash position, 13-week forecast, obligations, AI insights and financial decisions into one explainable workspace—so you can act before cash becomes a problem.
hero.belowCta: No credit card required. Demo companies included.

landing.benefitForecast: 13-week rolling cash forecast, updated from your bank accounts
landing.benefitRunway: Know your cash runway in weeks — not months
landing.benefitAlerts: Liquidity alerts when your position moves against you
landing.socialProof: Currently in private beta — 5 design partners onboarded

security.headline: Enterprise-Grade Security Built for Finance Teams
security.subheadline: Protecting sensitive financial data with the controls and governance expected by modern finance organizations.
security.bankGradeDesc: AES-256 encryption at rest and TLS 1.3 encryption in transit.
security.controlledAccessDesc: Role-based permissions across companies, entities and team members.
security.auditDesc: Every financial action is logged, traceable and reviewable.
security.residencyDesc: Keep financial data aligned with regional compliance requirements.

credibility.headline: Built by Finance Professionals Who Understand Cash Flow
credibility.body: Liqvia was designed by finance and audit professionals who experienced first-hand the limitations of spreadsheet-based cash management.

landing.signInHint: Access your company workspace with your existing account.
landing.registerHint: Create an account and set up your organization.
landing.demoHint: Try Liqvia with Acme Construction sample data — no sign-in needed.
landing.demoAltLabel: Or try a sample business closer to your own:
landing.partnerAvatarsCaption: Trusted by finance teams in private beta

cashPosition.title: Live cash position
cashPosition.description: Consolidated balances across operating and reserve accounts, updated from your uploads.

cashRunway.title: Cash runway in weeks
cashRunway.description: See how many weeks of liquidity remain at your current burn rate.

liquidityAlerts.title: Liquidity alerts
liquidityAlerts.description: Get notified when runway tightens or payables threaten your cash position.

forecastMethodology.headline: Transparent 13-week cash forecasts — not a black box
forecastMethodology.intro: Liqvia builds your forecast from the same AR, AP, bank, and weekly actual data your finance team already maintains. Every rule is documented, testable, and visible in the app.

howItWorks.title: How forecasts are generated
howItWorks.body: Each upload refreshes a rolling weekly cash model tied to your company’s opening balance.
howItWorks.b1: Start from opening cash (bank balances) and your uploaded AR/AP ageing.
howItWorks.b2: Schedule inflows on receivable due dates and outflows on payable due dates, week by week.
howItWorks.b3: Layer in rolling burn from recent weekly actuals, then recalculate runway and liquidity status.

assumptions.title: Forecast assumptions
assumptions.body: Assumptions follow standard SME treasury practice and match what you see in the product.
assumptions.b1: Overdue receivables are assumed to collect in week 1; future items follow stated due dates.
assumptions.b2: Payables respect supplier priority — payroll and tax before flexible suppliers.
assumptions.b3: When uploaded, weekly actuals extend the forecast using a recent-week average (default 4 weeks).

accuracy.title: Accuracy & validation
accuracy.body: We focus on explainability and continuous improvement — not opaque accuracy claims.
accuracy.b1: Core treasury rules are covered by automated unit tests with fixed numeric examples.
accuracy.b2: Upload prior-period budget and weekly actuals to compare forecast drivers vs reality in Budget vs Actual.
accuracy.b3: Use Scenarios to stress-test assumptions — and check the in-app backtest on Cash Forecast once weekly actuals are uploaded.

scenarioExamples.headline: Answer “what if?” in plain English
scenarioExamples.intro: Stress-test your baseline forecast with sliders — no spreadsheet rebuild. Start from real SME questions, then see week-13 cash and runway side by side.
scenarioExamples.sliderHint: In Scenarios: apply “{label}” or fine-tune the sliders yourself.

revenue_drop_20.question: What if revenue drops 20%?
revenue_drop_20.model: Liqvia reduces expected receivable collections by 20% and recalculates your 13-week closing cash and runway.

hire_three.question: What if we hire 3 people?
hire_three.model: Model hiring as a payroll cost increase — e.g. +12% payroll on your current team — and see how many weeks of cash remain.

payments_slow_15.question: What if customer payments slow by 15 days?
payments_slow_15.model: Liqvia shifts receivable collection dates 15 days later, showing the cash gap before collections land.

signedIn.title: Welcome back, {name}
signedIn.subtitle: Continue to your treasury dashboard or finish company setup.

forecast.pageSubtitle: Weekly inflow/outflow projection with version locking.
forecast.gridHint: Inflows and outflows are driven by live AR/AP due dates. Ledger entries are highlighted.

assumptionsPanel.title: Forecast assumptions
assumptionsPanel.subtitle: How this forecast is built — same rules as the marketing methodology page.

howItWorks.title: How forecasts are generated
howItWorks.body: Each upload refreshes a rolling weekly cash model tied to your opening balance.
howItWorks.b1: Start from opening cash and uploaded AR/AP ageing.
howItWorks.b2: Schedule inflows on receivable due dates and outflows on payable due dates.
howItWorks.b3: Layer rolling burn from recent weekly actuals, then recalculate runway.

assumptions.title: Key assumptions
assumptions.body: Standard SME treasury rules — visible and testable.
assumptions.b1: Overdue receivables are assumed to collect in week 1 — see Risk insights for exposure; use Scenarios to stress-test delays.
assumptions.b2: Payables respect supplier priority tiers from your AP upload — payroll and tax first.
assumptions.b3: Weekly actuals extend the forecast using a recent-week average (default 4 weeks).

accuracy.title: Accuracy & backtesting
accuracy.body: We show historical error metrics when enough weekly actuals exist.
accuracy.b1: Rolling net-cash backtest compares prior-week averages to actual weekly net cash.
accuracy.b2: Budget variance MAPE uses uploaded prior-period budget vs weekly actuals.
accuracy.b3: See the backtest card below once you have at least a few weeks of actuals.

backtest.title: Historical backtest
backtest.subtitle: How well a simple rolling-average model would have predicted recent weekly net cash — this is forecast accuracy vs actuals, not a scenario stress test.
backtest.insufficientHint: Upload weekly actuals (and prior-period budget for variance MAPE) to unlock backtest metrics.
backtest.netErrorHint: Average absolute difference vs actual weekly net cash
backtest.netErrorPctHint: Percent error where actual net cash was non-zero
backtest.budgetErrorPctHint: From {count} budget vs actual lines in the 14-week window

riskInsights.title: Forecast risk insights
riskInsights.subtitle: Read-only analysis of key assumptions — baseline forecast numbers are unchanged.
riskInsights.arBody: The baseline forecast schedules cash on due dates. Overdue invoices are assumed to collect in week 1, which can be optimistic for construction and other slow-paying sectors.
riskInsights.scheduledOnDueHint: Not yet overdue — scheduled by due date within the horizon
riskInsights.weightedExpectationHint: 70/20/10 age-weighted view — not used in the baseline forecast
riskInsights.avgOverdueDaysHint: Average lateness of overdue open invoices
riskInsights.overdueWarning: If overdue collections slip, up to {amount} may not arrive in week 1. Use Scenarios → receivable delay to stress-test.
riskInsights.apBody: Supplier Priority from your AP upload (payroll → tax → critical → flexible → non-essential). All payables still schedule on due dates — priority affects scenario stress, not baseline timing.
riskInsights.payrollGapWarning: Week-1 payroll due ({payroll}) exceeds opening cash plus week-1 inflows by {gap} — review liquidity.
riskInsights.burnBody: From weekly actuals using a {weeks}-week lookback (same driver as recurring forecast outflows).
riskInsights.burnTrendHint: Compares recent half vs earlier half of the lookback window
riskInsights.scenarioCta: Stress-test collection delays and cost changes in

freeCashBreakdown.title: Free cash after known outflows
freeCashBreakdown.subtitle: How the {horizon}-week free cash figure is built from your live forecast.
freeCashBreakdown.explainer: This is current bank cash minus outflows already modelled in your baseline forecast — not a separate calculation.
freeCashBreakdown.noteAp: Scheduled payables = AP ageing due dates within the forecast horizon.
freeCashBreakdown.noteRecurring: Recurring outflows = rolling average from weekly actuals (when uploaded).
freeCashBreakdown.noteNotIncluded: Not included: unmodelled tax, loan principal, credit lines, or contingency buffers.

budget.noFilterResults: No rows match the selected filters.

ledger.pageSubtitle: Accounts receivable and payable with aging analysis.

dashboard.title: Cash overview
dashboard.subtitle: What's coming in, what's going out, and how much cash you can safely spend.
dashboard.freeCashHint: Current cash minus scheduled payables and recurring operating outflows over the selected {horizon}-week horizon. Does not include unmodelled tax, debt, or contingency buffers.
dashboard.freeCashHintAria: How free available cash is calculated

dashboard.alertsEmpty: No active alerts. Liquidity looks healthy.

scenario.title: Scenario modelling
scenario.subtitle: Stress-test your 13-week forecast with flexible presets, live preview, and saved comparisons.
scenario.previewHint: Type exact values or use sliders — forecast updates ~0.7s after you stop editing. Save to keep a named scenario.
scenario.resultHint: Compared to your current baseline forecast (same opening cash and uploaded data).
scenario.compareHint: Pin up to two saved scenarios to compare week-13 cash against your current slider preview.
scenario.readOnly: You have view-only access to scenarios.
scenario.presetSectionHint: Pick a preset by concern, fine-tune sliders, then save optimistic or pessimistic cases for side-by-side review.

saved.title: Saved scenarios
saved.subtitle: Load a prior run or pin up to two for comparison.
saved.empty: No saved scenarios yet — adjust sliders and click Save.

mechanics.title: How scenarios work
mechanics.subtitle: What each control changes, when it applies, and how to read the results.
mechanics.recalcBody: Type values or use sliders — preview recalculates after a short pause (~0.7s). Nothing is saved until you click Save scenario. Baseline forecast on Cash Forecast is never overwritten.

revenueDecline.label: Revenue decline (%)
revenueDecline.changes: Scales down the outstanding amount on every open receivable in your AR upload — including overdue invoices counted in week 1.
revenueDecline.timing: Prospective volume stress on today’s open AR. Does not rewrite historical weekly actuals.

revenueGrowth.label: Revenue growth (%)
revenueGrowth.changes: Scales up outstanding receivable amounts — useful for optimistic or upsell scenarios.
revenueGrowth.timing: Prospective on open AR only; combines with revenue decline if both are set.

payrollIncrease.label: Payroll increase (%)
payrollIncrease.changes: Increases outstanding amounts on payables tagged as payroll in your AP upload.
payrollIncrease.timing: Prospective — affects scheduled payroll outflows from today forward. Rolling payroll from weekly actuals is unchanged.

receivableDelay.label: Receivable delay (days)
receivableDelay.changes: Shifts invoice and due dates on open receivables forward — collections move into later forecast weeks (similar to slower DSO).
receivableDelay.timing: Prospective timing shift only; AR amounts stay the same unless revenue decline/growth is also set.

payableDelay.label: Payable deferral (days)
payableDelay.changes: Shifts AP due dates forward — payments move to later weeks (short-term cash relief).
payableDelay.timing: Prospective timing shift on open AP; amounts unchanged unless expense/payroll/tax growth is also set.

expenseGrowth.label: Expense growth (%)
expenseGrowth.changes: Increases outstanding amounts on non-payroll, non-tax payables (suppliers, rent, etc.) in your AP upload.
expenseGrowth.timing: Prospective — scheduled supplier outflows rise. Recurring outflows inferred from weekly actuals are unchanged.

taxIncrease.label: Tax increase (%)
taxIncrease.changes: Increases outstanding amounts on payables tagged as tax in your AP upload.
taxIncrease.timing: Prospective — scheduled tax outflows rise independently of general expense growth.

oneOffInflow.label: One-off inflow (amount)
oneOffInflow.changes: Adds a lump-sum cash inflow in the chosen forecast week (e.g. equity, grant, asset sale).
oneOffInflow.timing: Single event in week N only; does not repeat.

oneOffInflowWeek.label: Inflow week (1–13)
oneOffInflowWeek.changes: Selects which forecast week receives the one-off inflow.

oneOffOutflow.label: One-off outflow (amount)
oneOffOutflow.changes: Adds a lump-sum cash outflow in the chosen week (e.g. capex, acquisition deposit).
oneOffOutflow.timing: Single event in week N only; does not repeat.

oneOffOutflowWeek.label: Outflow week (1–13)
oneOffOutflowWeek.changes: Selects which forecast week pays the one-off outflow.

mechanics.w13Title: How to read week-13 cash delta
mechanics.w13Body: Week-13 closing cash is the end point of the 13-week horizon using today’s opening cash and uploaded AR/AP. The delta is scenario minus baseline — negative means the stress test leaves you with less cash at the horizon. Runway delta uses the same weekly burn logic as the dashboard.
mechanics.unaffectedBody: Uploaded weekly actuals, bank opening balance logic, baseline Cash Forecast records, and historical backtest metrics. Scenarios are a parallel what-if layer.
mechanics.backtestBody: Historical backtest on Cash Forecast compares past weekly net cash to a simple rolling-average prediction — it measures forecast accuracy, not a scenario outcome. Use scenarios here for forward what-if; use backtest after uploading weekly actuals to judge historical fit.

headcountCalc.title: Headcount → payroll calculator
headcountCalc.subtitle: Convert hires and salary into a payroll increase % for the slider.
headcountCalc.usingPayrollData: Using estimated annual payroll from your weekly actuals: {amount}.
headcountCalc.usingTeamSize: Using team-size ratio (no payroll actuals found) — team of {size}.

ai.title: AI CFO Insight
ai.pageSubtitle: Ask plain-English questions about your cash, grounded in your live financial data.
ai.subtitle: Executive briefing powered by your live financial data.
ai.placeholder: e.g. What is my biggest liquidity risk this quarter?
ai.emptyChat: Ask a question or choose a quick action above. Responses use your current cash, runway, AR/AP, and budget data.
ai.sourceRuleApiError: Rule-based briefing (AI request failed — using fallback)
ai.disclosureBanner: Not a general-purpose AI: answers are generated by rule-based templates (or GPT when configured) applied strictly to your uploaded data — always verify important decisions against the source records.

aiQuick.runway: What is my current cash runway?
aiQuick.expenses: Show my top 5 expenses this month.
aiQuick.suppliers: Which suppliers require payment this week?
aiQuick.risks: What financial risks should I be aware of?

bankAccounts.emptyHint: Upload bank balances or cash movements to populate accounts.
bankAccounts.reconciliationHint: Heuristic match against open AR/AP by amount and date — review before relying on it, not a certified reconciliation.
bankAccounts.purposeSubtitle: Tell Liqvia what each account is used for so your cash position is easier to understand.

settings.subtitle: Company profile, team access, and chart of accounts.
settings.field_forecastLookbackHint: Average of the last 1–4 weeks of actuals used for the 13-week cash forecast and rolling budget.
settings.field_reportingPeriodHint: Used for budget variance, forecast context, and upload analysis. Monthly: YYYY-MM. Weekly: YYYY-W##.
settings.coaEmptyHint: Create accounts to categorize transactions and budgets.
settings.entitiesEmptyHint: Create a new entity to manage a separate company workspace under your account.
settings.entitiesBankAccountsHint: Optional — add bank accounts and balances without uploading a file.
settings.entitiesSwitchAfterCreate: Switch to this entity after creation
settings.entitiesPermissionHint: Switch between entities here. Your role controls what you can change in Settings → Team for each entity.
settings.teamRoleHint: Change roles for the active entity. Switch entity first, then update permissions in this tab.
settings.accessMatrixAdminOnly: The full role matrix is only visible to administrators.
settings.accessMatrixSubtitle: What each role can do across treasury, uploads, scenarios, and administration.

owner.label: Owner
owner.summary: Full control over the workspace, team, and settings.

admin.label: Admin

uploader.label: Uploader
uploader.summary: Upload files only — no treasury dashboards or settings.

recurringObligations.emptyHint: Add payroll, rent, tax, or loan repayments that recur on a schedule so forecasts and AI CFO account for them.

businessPulse.title: Today's Business Pulse
businessPulse.subtitle: The most important cash flow issues that need your attention.
businessPulse.allClear: Nothing urgent today — everything's on track.
businessPulse.payrollOverdueMessage: {name}: {amount} is overdue by {days} day(s) (due {date}). This should usually be treated as the highest payment priority.
businessPulse.payrollOverdueAction: Process payroll immediately, or communicate clearly with employees if there is a delay.
businessPulse.supplierOverdueMessage: {name}: {amount} is overdue by {days} day(s) (due {date}). Further delays may affect supplier relationships, future deliveries, or service continuity.
businessPulse.supplierOverdueAction: Prioritise critical suppliers first and defer non-essential payments where possible.
businessPulse.collectMessage: {name}: {amount} is overdue by {days} day(s) (due {date}). Collecting this could improve cash flow without reducing business activity.
businessPulse.collectAction: Contact {name} and confirm an expected payment date.
businessPulse.obligationDueMessage: {amount} is due in {days} day(s) ({date}). Missing this could disrupt operations or incur penalties.
businessPulse.obligationDueTodayMessage: {amount} is due today ({date}). Missing this could disrupt operations or incur penalties.
businessPulse.obligationDueAction: Make sure funds are set aside to cover this payment on time.
businessPulse.expectingMessage: {amount} is expected in {days} day(s) (due {date}), which should support your cash position.
businessPulse.expectingAction: No action needed — this is a positive signal, not a risk.
businessPulse.noBufferMessage: Based on current cash and expected obligations, free available cash is {amount} — the business may not have enough after planned payments.
businessPulse.noBufferAction: Review upcoming payments, delay non-essential spending, and accelerate customer collections.
businessPulse.thinBufferMessage: After expected obligations, the business has {amount} of cash cushion left, covering about {weeks} week(s). Unexpected expenses could create cash pressure.
businessPulse.thinBufferAction: Delay discretionary spending until the cash buffer improves.
businessPulse.forecastShortfallTitle: Cash shortfall expected in forecast period
businessPulse.forecastShortfallMessage: The forecast shows cash may fall to {amount} in week {week} (around {date}), below a safe level.
businessPulse.forecastShortfallAction: Identify the week of pressure and adjust payments, collections, or spending before then.
businessPulse.staleBankMessage: Recent bank transactions haven't been updated in {days} day(s), which can reduce forecast accuracy.
businessPulse.staleBankAction: Upload or refresh bank transactions before relying on the forecast.
businessPulse.payrollRiskTitle: Payroll may not be fully covered
businessPulse.payrollRiskMessage: Based on current available cash and expected payroll, the business may not have enough cash set aside for the next payroll run.
businessPulse.payrollRiskAction: Transfer funds into the payroll account or delay non-essential payments before payroll is due.
businessPulse.directDebitPressureTitle: Upcoming direct debits may reduce cash buffer
businessPulse.directDebitPressureMessage: Several fixed payments are due soon and may reduce available operating cash.
businessPulse.directDebitPressureAction: Review upcoming direct debits and make sure the linked accounts have enough funds.
businessPulse.settlementDelayMessage: An expected funding or payment processor settlement has not been matched to recent bank transactions.
businessPulse.settlementDelayAction: Check the payment platform or government portal and confirm the expected payment date.
businessPulse.cashInSettlementTitle: Cash may be sitting in settlement accounts
businessPulse.cashInSettlementMessage: Some cash appears to be held in merchant, Amex, NDIS, or settlement accounts rather than the main operating account.
businessPulse.cashInSettlementAction: Consider transferring surplus funds into the operating or payroll account.
businessPulse.lowOperatingCashMessage: The main operating account may not have enough cash to cover near-term expenses, even though total cash across all accounts may look healthy.
businessPulse.lowOperatingCashAction: Review balances by account purpose and transfer funds where needed.

cashDriven.noPayrollObligation: No payroll obligation set up yet.
cashDriven.noObligations: No recurring obligations set up yet.
cashDriven.noSettlements: No expected settlements set up yet.
cashDriven.availableToSpendHint: Not all cash is freely available — reserves and in-transit settlement funds are set aside.

decisionCentre.title: Can I...?
decisionCentre.error: Couldn't get an answer — please try again.

messages.moduleStaleImpact: Forecast and AI CFO answers may be based on outdated figures.
messages.moduleMissingImpact: Anything relying on this module is a rough estimate, not a real figure.
messages.moduleMissingFix: Upload data for {module} to enable this.
messages.noBankAccountsImpact: Cash position is based on manual entry only, not real transactions.
messages.noBankAccountsFix: Connect or add at least one bank account.
messages.historyWeeksLowImpact: Forecasts and burn-rate estimates are less reliable with limited history.
messages.historyWeeksLowFix: Upload more historical bank transactions or weekly actuals.
messages.taxObligationsModeled: Payroll and tax obligations are modeled as recurring commitments.
messages.noTaxObligations: No payroll, GST, PAYG, or super obligations configured.
messages.noTaxObligationsImpact: The forecast can miss large, predictable outflows before they hit the bank feed.
messages.noTaxObligationsFix: Add recurring obligations for payroll and tax in Settings → Recurring Obligations.
messages.budgetInPlace: A budget is in place to compare actuals against.
messages.noBudgetImpact: There is nothing to check whether actuals are tracking to plan.
messages.noBudgetFix: Upload a budget to enable budget-vs-actual comparison.
messages.allGood: Keep uploads current — this workspace is in good shape.

dashboardWidgets.subtitle: Choose what appears on your dashboard — everyone on your team can set this differently.

company.title: Company Details
company.name: Company Name
company.bankAccountsHint: Add your bank accounts and current balances here — no file upload required. You can skip the bank balances upload step later.

businessMode.question: How does this business mainly manage incoming and outgoing cash?
businessMode.option_invoice_driven: Invoice-based business — We issue invoices and track receivables/payables.
businessMode.option_cash_driven: Cash-driven business — We mainly rely on recurring income, settlements, payroll, and direct debits.
businessMode.option_mixed: Mixed business — We use both invoices and recurring/settlement-based cash flows.

team.title: Your Team
team.subtitle: Invite colleagues to your workspace. You can skip this step and add people later.

upload.title: Upload your data
upload.subtitle: Upload CSV or Excel files using our templates. Bank accounts and balances can be entered manually on the company step — uploads here are optional.
upload.skipHint: All uploads are optional. Skip any you already entered manually or plan to add later.

preview.subtitle: Confirm everything looks right before finishing setup.

done.title: You're all set
done.subtitle: Your treasury workspace is ready. We've generated your initial cash forecast.

upload.title: Financial data upload center
upload.subtitle: Upload 14 weeks of actuals and prior-period budget for variance; optional forward rolling budget.
upload.cashDrivenBanner: You do not need AR/AP ageing reports to use Liqvia in Cash-Driven Mode. Upload your bank balances, recurring expenses, payroll schedule, and expected settlement dates instead.
upload.rollingHint: Variance uses prior-period budget vs actuals; forward rolling budget is optional.
upload.rollingStep1: Upload Weekly Actuals — past 14 weeks by category (YYYY-W##).
upload.rollingStep2: Upload Prior Period Budget — same 14 weeks for variance analysis vs actuals.
upload.rollingStep3: Variance compares prior-period budget against weekly actuals in that window.
upload.rollingStep4: Rolling Budget (optional) — upload next 13 weeks, or leave blank to auto-derive from prior period (or actuals if no budget uploaded).
upload.selectTemplateHint: Each template has strict column names and validation rules.
upload.templatesSectionHint: Use these CSV or Excel files as starting points. Column headers must match exactly.
upload.templatesCollapsedHint: Click to expand CSV and Excel templates for every data type.
upload.dropzone: Click to select a CSV or Excel file
upload.unsupportedFile: Unsupported file type. Upload a CSV or Excel (.xlsx, .xls) file.
upload.historyHint: Click any upload to view the stored snapshot. Latest per type is highlighted above.
upload.latestHint: Most recent completed import for each data type — click to view the full snapshot
upload.noSnapshotDetail: This upload was imported before snapshots were stored. Re-import to retain a reference copy.
upload.wipeConfirm: Clear all live financial data? Upload history will be kept for reference.
upload.activeDataHint: Live records currently powering dashboards and analysis
upload.activeDataEmpty: No live data for this type — upload a file to import.
upload.readOnly: You can review upload history and templates, but only uploaders and admins can import files.
upload.deleteUploadConfirm: Remove this upload from history? Live data is unchanged because a newer import exists.
upload.deleteUploadConfirmLatest: Remove this upload and its live data? The previous version will be restored automatically if one exists.
upload.clearActiveConfirm: Clear live data for this type? Upload history will be kept.

ai.title: AI Upload Centre
ai.subtitle: Import AR ageing, AP ageing, bank transactions, expense reports, weekly actuals, or bank balances from any CSV, Excel, or PDF export — mapped into Liqvia’s canonical templates.
ai.genericNote: Select the target data type, upload exports as-is, and we detect columns and map them to the required template.
ai.signConventionNote: For bank transactions: most bank reports use minus (−) for credits (money in) and plus (+) for debits (money out). We normalize to positive Amount + IN/OUT Direction.
ai.standardUploadLink: Use the standard Upload Center for pre-formatted templates.
ai.uploadHint: Upload one or more exports as-is (CSV, Excel, or PDF). Mix formats freely — we detect columns, map fields, and merge into one import.
ai.dropzone: Click to select files or drag them here
ai.multiFileHint: Select multiple files — CSV, Excel, and PDF can be mixed in one batch (up to 20 files).
ai.defaultAccountPlaceholder: Used when the file has no account column
ai.validationFailed: Normalized rows failed Liqvia validation — review errors below.

templateHints.bank_transactions: Bank statement or transaction export — powers cash forecast when AR/AP ageing is unavailable.
templateHints.weekly_actuals: 14 weeks of revenue, payroll, and expense actuals by ISO week.
templateHints.bank_balances: Current bank account balances as of a snapshot date.

validationSpec.title: Data validation rules
validationSpec.subtitle: What we reject on upload — invalid rows block import; duplicates and bad amounts never reach the forecast.
validationSpec.footer: Credit memos and future-dated invoices are not yet modelled separately — use positive amounts and correct due dates. Retainage is not a field today; adjust amounts manually if needed.

duplicates.title: Duplicate detection
duplicates.body: AR: duplicate Invoice Number. AP: duplicate Bill Number. Budget/actuals: duplicate Period + Category + Account Code. Bank: duplicate account + date + amount + direction.

amounts.title: Amount rules
amounts.body: AR/AP outstanding amounts must be greater than zero. Zero or negative rows are rejected. Weekly actuals may be signed (expenses negative).

dates.title: Date rules
dates.body: Due date must be on or after invoice/bill date. ISO format YYYY-MM-DD required.

currency.title: Currency match
currency.body: AR, AP, and bank balance rows must match your company currency.

periodWindow.title: Period windows
periodWindow.body: Weekly actuals and prior budget must fall in the past 14 weeks. Rolling budget must fall in the next 13 weeks.

supplierPriority.title: Supplier priority (AP)
supplierPriority.body: Required on AP uploads: payroll, tax, critical, flexible, or non-essential. Used in scenario stress and risk breakdown — baseline forecast still schedules by due date.

upload.requiredColumnsFileHint: Exact column names in your CSV/Excel file: {headers}
